channel.title: public-archive on mahmood
channel.description: Recent content in public-archive on mahmood

item.title: dot product
item.description: the dot product of two vectors is (JOHN H. HUBBARD, BARBARA BURKE HUBBARD, 2015 definition 1.4.1) the dot product of two vectors can be written as the matrix product of the transpose of one vector by the other: (JOHN H. HUBBARD, BARBARA BURKE HUBBARD, 2015 chapter 1 vectors, matrices, and derivatives) the dot product is commutative. the scalar product of 2 vectors A and B is defined as where is the angle between A and B when they are drawn tail to tail (to eliminate ambiguity, is always taken as the angle smaller than ): when the vectors are in the form of a list of components, e.

item.title: inner product
item.description: an inner product on a vector space is an operation on pairs of vectors in that satisfies the same conditions that the dot product in euclidean space does: namely, bilinearity, symmetry, and positive definiteness. a vector space equipped with an inner product is an inner product space. (Joseph A. Gallian, 2021 chapter 1 inner product spaces) if , we define the inner product to be the quantity (terence tao, 2023 definition 5.

item.title: set
item.description: we define a set to be any unordered collection of objects, e.g., is a set. if is an object, we say that is an element of or if lies in the collection; otherwise we say that . for instance, but . (terence tao, 2022 chapter 3 set theory) let be a set. for any object , and any object , suppose we have a statement pertaining to and , such that for each there is at most one for which is true.

item.title: common lisp lparallel
item.description: lparallel is a multithreading library for common lisp, it is useful because the ANSI CL standard doesnt have multithreading it is available via quicklisp: (ql:quickload :lparallel) to get the number of cores we have to use the serapeum package: (ql:quickload :serapeum) usage: (serapeum:count-cpus) 16 in the context of lparallel, a kernel is an abstract entity that schedules and executes tasks. the lparallel kernel API is meant to describe parallelism in a generic manner.

item.title: cuda programming in common lisp
item.description: load it using quicklisp: (ql:quickload :cl-cuda) arch linux setup i had to install cuda for the nvcc compiler and more, and nvidia for the gpu driver at first you may have to use /opt/cuda/bin/nvcc until you reboot or extend your $PATH to check which nvidia driver is in use run: lspci -k | grep -A 2 -E "(VGA|3D)" if you have installed the nvidia package and your os still uses nouveau you need to reboot if it still doesnt use nvidia you need to update your while system with pacman -Syyu (you’ve been doing partial upgrades) essential concepts threads mostly from https://docs.

item.title: linearly mapping an interval onto another
item.description: basically the idea is that, given an real interval and the number such that and another interval , we wanna find the number such that we need to isolate : extending to higher dimensions assume a vector space with a dimension of , given the 2 sets of real intervals and a vector : where for all is bound in the interval , i.e. we wanna find the vector : such that so we write the linear transformation (might not be necessarily linear but affine) obviously this function doesnt preserve the origin as , so its an affine transformation, we separate the intercept so we can drop it and add it later: we drop the intercept and find the transformation matrix of the function without it, which is a square matrix of size im taking it for granted that the without the intercept is linear, in reality we need to check if the properties of a linear function are preserved in then we add the intercept with homogeneous coordinates to get the final matrix, which is a square matrix of size so to conclude, the formula transforms the vector from a given set of intervals onto another, resulting in

item.title: 2d transformations
item.description: we usually use a vector to represent a point in 2d space a general form of linear transformations (or more precisely, affine transformations) in can be wrriten as: or or, in matrix form, using homogeneous coordinates: matrices allow arbitrary linear transformations to be displayed in a consistent format, suitable for computation 2d translation given a point , we wanna reposition it with the translation distance this is an affine transformation, we deduce an affine transformation matrix: 2d rotation about the origin a rotation about the origin by angle can be done with the matrix: 2d scaling scaling is just multiplying the variables by some constants, it is defined using the cross product of the point by the scaling vector we deduce the transformation matrix: using homogeneous coordinates: linearly mapping a grid to another simplifying the formula from linearly mapping an interval onto another to 2 dimensions: mapping the point from the intervals , respectively, to the intervals , respectively

item.title: logic formulas
item.description: note that and represent true and false, respectively propositional logic equivalence number equivalence rule name E1 E2 E3 E4 E5 E6 E7 E8 E9 E10 E11 E12 E13 E14 distributivity E15 distributivity E16 de morgan E17 de morgan E18 E19 E20 E21 E22 E23 logical implication number implication rule name I1 I2 I3 I4 I5 I6 I7 I8 I9 I10 I11 I12 I13 I14 first-order logic logical implication number implication I'1 I'2 I'3 I'4 I'5 I'6 I'7 I'8 I'9 I'10 rule of inference name rule of inference Universal Instantiation where is some arbitrary element of the universe Universal Generalization where holds for every element of the universe Existential Instantiation where is some element of the universe of discourse.

item.title: cross-entropy
item.description: cross-entropy, written as , is a kind of measure of dissimilarity between two distribution and . the general definition is: (Peter Norvig, Stuart J. Russell, 2020) let and be two densities on (or any other interval). the cross-entropy of with respect to is defined by the expectation with respect to of the negative likelihood function as this represents the information given by assessed from the point of view of distribution , which is supposed to be given.

item.title: decision tree
item.description: (Peter Norvig, Stuart J. Russell, 2020) a decision tree represents a function that takes as input a vector of attribute values and returns a “decision”–a single output value. the input and output values can be discrete or continuous for a wide variety of problems, the decision tree yields a nice, concise result. but some functions cannot be represented concisely, for example, the majority function, which returns true if and only if more than half of the inputs are true, requires an exponentially large decision tree.

item.title: joint probability distribution
item.description: given two random variables that are defined on the same probability space, the joint probability distribution is the corresponding probability distribution on all possible pairs of outputs. it is denoted by for any , let be a real random variable. for any finite subset , let then is called the joint distribution function of . the probability measure on is called the joint distribution of . (Achim Klenke, 2020 definition 2.

item.title: probability distribution
item.description: the probability distribution is a function that gives the probabilities of different outcomes for an experiment. if is a discrete random variable then the list of values that it could receive and their corresponding probabilities are called the distribution law, probability law, or the distribution of said variable let be a random variable. the probability measure is called the distribution of . for a real random variable , the map is called the distribution function of (or, more accurately, of ).

item.title: 2d rotation about the origin
item.description: a rotation of a point about the origin through an angle maps it to another point such that p and and p’ are at the same distance from the origin and the angle from the vector p to the vector p’ is to determine the coordinates of the image point , it is very convenient to use polar coordinates. let , where is the distance from to the origin and the polar angle.
